questions.question: What is the highest mountain peak of Armenia?
questions.info: Aragats is the highest peak of Armenia at 4090 meters.

questions.question: In what year was the city of Yerevan founded?
questions.info: Yerevan was founded in 782 BC with the construction of the Erebuni fortress.

questions.question: In what year was Armenia's independence declared?
questions.info: Armenia's independence was declared on September 21, 1991.

questions.question: In what year was Christianity declared the state religion in Armenia?
questions.info: Christianity was declared the state religion in Armenia in 301 AD.

questions.question: Who created the Armenian alphabet?
questions.info: The Armenian alphabet was created by Mesrop Mashtots in 405 AD.

questions.question: Under which king did Armenia reach its peak of power?
questions.info: Under Tigran the Great, Armenia stretched from the Mediterranean Sea to the Caspian Sea.

questions.question: Who is considered the father of Armenian historiography?
questions.info: Movses Khorenatsi is considered the father of Armenian historiography.

questions.question: In which city was the First Republic of Armenia proclaimed?
questions.info: The First Republic of Armenia was proclaimed in Tiflis on May 28, 1918.

questions.question: In which museum is the cuneiform inscription of Erebuni's foundation kept?
questions.info: The cuneiform inscription of Erebuni's foundation is kept in the Erebuni Museum.

questions.question: Who was one of the great Armenian writers of the 19th century whose works greatly influenced national consciousness?
questions.info: Raffi was a 19th-century Armenian writer whose historical novels greatly influenced national consciousness.

questions.question: Which catholicos is known for his Armenian translations and educational work?
questions.info: Sahak Partev played a major role in the spread of Armenian writing and the translation movement in the 5th century.

questions.question: Which battle prevented the Turkish invasion of Yerevan in 1918?
questions.info: The Battle of Sardarapat in 1918 was decisive in ensuring Armenia's existence.

questions.question: Under which king was the Temple of Garni built?
questions.info: The pagan Temple of Garni was built under King Trdat I.

questions.question: Which Armenian commander participated in the restoration of the Bagratuni kingdom?
questions.info: Ashot I Bagratuni was proclaimed king in 885, restoring the Armenian kingdom.

questions.question: What was the capital of the Armenian Kingdom of Cilicia?
questions.info: The capital of Cilicia was the city of Sis.

questions.question: Which Armenian commander led the liberation struggle in Syunik in the 18th century?
questions.info: Davit Bek led the liberation struggle in Syunik in the 1720s.

questions.question: In which city was the Treaty of Sèvres signed?
questions.info: The Treaty of Sèvres was signed in 1920 in the city of Sèvres.

questions.question: Who was the Prime Minister of the First Republic of Armenia?
questions.info: The first Prime Minister of Armenia was Hovhannes Kajaznuni.

questions.question: Who is the great Armenian poet called the 'Poet of All Armenians'?
questions.info: Hovhannes Tumanyan is considered the Poet of All Armenians.

questions.question: Which organization recognized Armenia's independence in December 1991?
questions.info: The CIS recognized Armenia's independence in December 1991.

questions.question: What was the name of the joint Soviet-Azerbaijani operation in May-June 1991 aimed at emptying Armenian villages of Artsakh?
questions.info: The operation is known as 'Ring', during which residents of Armenian villages were forcibly deported.

questions.question: Which international format was created in 1992 for the peaceful resolution of the Karabakh conflict?
questions.info: The OSCE Minsk Group was created in 1992 as a negotiation platform.

questions.question: In the capital of which country was the 1994 ceasefire signed?
questions.info: The ceasefire was signed in Bishkek, the capital of Kyrgyzstan.

questions.question: Who was the Minister of Defense of Armenia at the time of the victory in the First Artsakh War?
questions.info: At the time of the final victory, the Minister of Defense of Armenia was Vazgen Sargsyan.

questions.question: Which Armenian commander is most famous for the Battle of Martakert?
questions.info: In the Battle of Martakert, the Armenian forces were commanded by Samvel Babayan.

questions.question: In what year and where was Monte Melkonian killed?
questions.info: Monte Melkonian was killed on June 12, 1993, in the Martuni region.

questions.question: Which military commander led the operation to liberate Shushi?
questions.info: The liberation of Shushi was led by Arkady Ter-Tadevosyan (Komandos).

questions.question: What was the code name for the Shushi liberation operation?
questions.info: The operation to liberate Shushi had the code name 'Wedding in the Mountains'.

questions.question: Which newspaper became one of the important media outlets of the Karabakh movement in Armenia?
questions.info: The 'Hayk' newspaper played a major role in spreading the ideas of the Artsakh movement.

questions.question: Who was the first president of the Nagorno-Karabakh Republic?
questions.info: Robert Kocharyan was elected the first president of the Nagorno-Karabakh Republic.

questions.question: Which commander was known for his 'Artsivner' (Eagles) detachment?
questions.info: Manvel Grigoryan commanded the 'Artsivner' detachment.

questions.question: What is the official date of the formation of the Artsakh Defense Army?
questions.info: The Artsakh Defense Army was officially formed in 1992.

questions.question: In which Azerbaijani city were large military forces concentrated, which were later neutralized by the Armenian side?
questions.info: Aghdam was the main base of Azerbaijani forces.

questions.question: In which Azerbaijani city were large military forces concentrated, which were later neutralized by the Armenian side?
questions.info: Aghdam was the main base of Azerbaijani forces.

questions.question: In which ancient capital is King Ashot the Iron buried?
questions.info: King Ashot the Iron is buried in the city of Dvin.

questions.question: Which is the largest province of Armenia by area?
questions.info: Syunik province is the largest in Armenia by area.

questions.question: Who is considered the 'Father of Armenian natural sciences'?
questions.info: Anania Shirakatsi is considered one of the founders of Armenian natural sciences and astronomy.

questions.question: What is the national epic of the Armenian people?
questions.info: The national epic of Armenia is 'Daredevils of Sassoun'.

questions.question: Which is the highest mountain range in Armenia?
questions.info: The Zangezur mountain range is the highest in Armenia.

questions.question: Which Armenian artist is a world-famous marine painter?
questions.info: Hovhannes Aivazovsky is a world-famous marine painter.

questions.question: Which is the highest lake in Armenia?
questions.info: Lake Kari is the highest lake in Armenia, located in the Geghama mountains.

questions.question: What is the first printed Armenian book?
questions.info: The first printed Armenian book is 'Urbatagirk', printed in Venice in 1512.

questions.question: Which Armenian commander led the Battle of Sardarapat?
questions.info: The Battle of Sardarapat was commanded by General Movses Silikyan.

questions.question: In what year was the first Constitution of Armenia adopted?
questions.info: The first Constitution of Armenia was adopted on July 5, 1995.

questions.question: In which language was Grigor Narekatsi's 'Book of Lamentations' written?
questions.info: Grigor Narekatsi's 'Book of Lamentations' was written in Grabar (Classical Armenian).

questions.question: Who was the Prime Minister of the First Republic of Armenia?
questions.info: The first Prime Minister of the First Republic was Hovhannes Kajaznuni.

questions.question: In what year was the Yerablur pantheon founded?
questions.info: Yerablur was founded in 1994 to commemorate the heroes of the First Artsakh War.

questions.question: What was the name of the leader of the Armenian volunteer detachments during World War I?
questions.info: Andranik Ozanian was the most famous commander of the Armenian volunteer movement.

questions.question: Who recorded and first published the 'Sasna Tsrer' epic?
questions.info: Garegin Srvandztiants recorded and published the 'Sasna Tsrer' epic in the 19th century.

questions.question: In what year did the Spitak earthquake occur?
questions.info: The devastating Spitak earthquake occurred on December 7, 1988.

questions.question: Who was the Artsakh War hero Ashot Ghulyan, known as:
questions.info: Ashot Ghulyan, a volunteer, was known by the name 'Bekor'.

questions.question: In which era was the Mother Cathedral of Ejmiatsin built?
questions.info: The Mother Cathedral of Ejmiatsin was built in the 4th century on the initiative of Gregory the Illuminator.

questions.question: What was the first university in medieval Armenia?
questions.info: The University of Gladzor was founded in the 13th century and is considered the first university in Armenia.

questions.question: What was the first university in medieval Armenia?
questions.info: The University of Gladzor was founded in the 13th century and is considered the first university in Armenia.

questions.question: Which Armenian monastery is included in the UNESCO World Heritage List?
questions.info: Haghpat Monastery in Lori is included in the UNESCO list.

questions.question: In what year was Yerevan State University founded?
questions.info: YSU was founded in 1919.

questions.question: Who is one of the composers of the music for 'Our Father'?
questions.info: Komitas is the greatest representative of Armenian sacred music.

questions.question: In which year did the Armenian national football team play its first match?
questions.info: The Armenian national football team played its first match in 1992.

questions.question: What is the order of colors of the RA state flag from top to bottom?
questions.info: The RA flag from top to bottom: red, blue, orange.

questions.question: In which city is the Sardarapat memorial complex located?
questions.info: The Sardarapat memorial is located in the Armavir province.

questions.question: In what year was the 'Hayastan' All-Armenian Fund founded?
questions.info: The 'Hayastan' All-Armenian Fund was founded in 1992.
